REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (222-224 - 23rd Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der appeared by zoom for and with Dennis Tanaka (Petitioner) by zoom.
Gabriella Fracchia (listing Agent) by zoom.
Elizabeth Chang (Buyer's Agent) by zoom.
Annie Ye (Buyer) by zoom.

No one appeared to place an overbid. The sale was confirmed at $1,520,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 8, 2020. The Court intends to grant the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond is not required. The matter is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (125-21st Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:, Ernest Der, Esq., counsel for and with Dennis Tanaka, Trustee (appearing separately by video conference); Jeffrey Guan, agent for Xiang Feng Cao, possible bidder (appearing by video conference); Michele Leung, agent for and with Michael Lee, possible bidder (appearing separately by video conference). Present by video conferencing but not appearing: Gabriella Fracchia, agent for and with James Nunemacher, original proposed buyer (appearing separately by video conference).

The Court notes at the outset the purpose of the hearing: sale of the real property commonly known as 125 21st Avenue, San Francisco, California. Finding that the petition and related pleadings now appear to be in order, the Court commences sale of the property, reciting the general terms of the sale and the bid price of $2,047,000. The Court recites the first overbid at $2,149,850. Jeffrey Guan, on behalf of Xiang Feng Cao offers the first overbid. Michele Leung, on behalf of Michael Lee, offers further overbid of $2,160,000. After subsequent over bid in $10,000 increments, the Court confirms the sale at $2,210,000 to Mr. Guan on behalf of Xiang Feng Cao.

After further discussion, Mr. Der represents the cashier's check for the overbid deposit had been previously reviewed and confirmed. Mr. Der will prepare the Order of Sale for review by the Court and will draft the Overbid form for review, signature by the buyer, and filing with the Court.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Continued To Jan-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Continued To Jan-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Continued To Nov-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der and Seth Skootsky for Dennis Tanaka (Respondent/Trustee).
Michael Bassi for Marilyn Tanaka (Petitioner).
John DiAna appeared by telephone for Audrey Iwamura (Objector).

The Court noted that the issues between Dennis and Marilyn are nearly resolved, and that Audrey is looking for a little more time to continue to work on resolution. Mr. Bassi stated that Audrey agrees in principal, but there are some sticky issues that need to be resolved and that they are discussing settlement. Mr. DiAna stated that the present proposal has some problems, that they need time to work on that and that he is optimistic that the three Beneficiaries will be able to work this out. Mr. Bassi also stated that he and Mr. DiAna want responses from Mr. Der. Mr. Skootsky stated that they received a chart on September 9th, that they are working on those issues, that they will do the best they can with it and noted that there is a problem with some account statements that were available some time ago that are now missing. The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust and Appoint Successor Trustee, etc. and the Order to Show Cause are continued to November 26, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status statement is to be filed one week prior to the hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Continued To Nov-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Continued To Sep-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Dennis Tanaka (Successor Trustee).
Michael Bassi for Marilyn Tanaka (Petitioner).
John Diana appeared by telephone for Audrey Iwamura.

The Court stated that it has real concern with Mr. Tanaka getting this done, that it ordered an accounting, that it provided subsequent opportunity to get the accounting completed and that there still is no accounting. Mr. Der stated that he filed a declaration in response to the order to show cause, that they are slowly getting records from Union Bank, that some records still have not been provided and that they paid the fee for the production of the bank records. Mr. Der also stated that his Client sold one lot and that there is a proposal of settlement of all of the issues. Mr. Bassi expressed concern about there being no movement with the accounting, stated that Mr. Tanaka is quibbling about how far back he has to go, spoke of the expense to his Client to get records from the trust CPA and Mr. Tanaka using trust funds to defend the matter, stated that this is wearing down his Client and that they are asking for Mr. Tanaka to be removed. The Court raised the issue of Mr. Tanaka not being able to get records if removed. Mr. Diana stated that today his position is that there is no harm in allowing an additional 90-days to complete the accounting, that if the Trustee is removed today it will add more confusion and litigation to the case and he sees no value in that, and that if there is no completion of the accounting within 90-days his position is to join with Mr. Bassi and call for removal of Mr. Tanaka. Mr. Der stated that there are gaps in the accounting that were filled in with the CPA's register, that he can not account for something that Mr. Tanaka had not marshalled yet, that Mr. Tanaka did not take control of the assets until after his Mother passed away and that he will not have records for that nine month period of time, but he can provide the CPA's register. Mr. Der also stated that he thinks there is enough information for the parties to go to mediation. Mr. Bassi stated that he is not interested in mediation, and that he does not believe it will resolve the case based on Mr. Tanaka's position and what he has done. Mr. Diana stated that he does not feel they are ready for mediation, that there are some basic questions that need to be answered based on Mr. Tanaka's conduct, that there are some wholes in the accounting and that right now they would be in the dark going into mediation. Mr. Der stated that he will provide copies of the schedules to Mr. Diana so that he can look at it to see what's missing. The Court suggested that Mr. Bassi put in writing what his Client feels has not been provided. Mr. Bassi stated that he has an outstanding request for documents, that he intends to take the deposition of Mr. Tanaka and that he is opposed to a 90-day continuance. The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. and the Order to Show Cause are continued to September 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects the parties to talk to see if they can reach resolution and get to the point where information is being shared to see where things stand. The Court stated that if it concludes that Mr. Tanaka is not providing information, it will be inclined to remove him and if it finds that Mr. Tanaka has provided all that he can, this may be headed to trial.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Continued To Sep-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Continued To Jul-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi for Marilyn Tanaka.
Audrey Iwamura in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that Attorney Ernest Der is not present and passed the matter to the end of the calendar. The matter is recalled. Still no appearance by Mr. Der. Mr. Bassi stated that he met and conferred with Mr. Der and Jason Louie, that Mr. Der's Client provided an accounting, but not a full one, that it covered from January 2010 through December 31, 2018, that they discussed the issue of the accounting not starting from November 6, 2007 the commencement of Dennis Tanaka's trusteeship, and that he has asked for an accounting from that date and has not received anything. Mr. Bassi also stated that he started some discovery with Ms. Iwamura's Attorney, that he granted them an extension of time to respond and that the next day Mr. Louie substituted out. In addition, Mr. Bassi stated that discovery is still outstanding, that subpoenas have been issued, that he has not received records, and that he is moving it forward, but it is not ready for trial. The Court informs Ms. Iwamura that not having an Attorney does not excuse her from complying the Code of Civil Procedures with regard to responding to discovery and advised her to get an Attorney. The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to July 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will issue an order to show cause to Mr. Der and Mr. Tanaka as to why the March 1, 2019 order has not been complied with and why Dennis Tanaka should not be suspended or removed. The order to show cause is to include an order to appear to Ernest Der and Dennis Tanaka to appear on July 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Bassi is to prepare the
OSC/OTA.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Continued To Jun-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Continued To Apr-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Bassi for Marilyn Tanaka (Petitioner).
Jason Louie for and with Audrey Iwamura (Objector).
Ernest Der for Dennis Tanaka (Current Trustee/Objector).

Mr. Louie stated that the objections were filed yesterday. The Court stated that it had ordered an accounting. Mr. Der addressed the accounting stating that there are some gaps caused by missing bank statements around 2011, that he's afraid that they'll need to subpoena records, that 90% of the accounting is done, that he's talked to the preparer and wanted to offer both counsel a spreadsheet for the accounting that they do have, and that they can't get it to balance until they get the statements. Mr. Der requested a 30-60 day continuance, stated that he's pushed his client harder than anyone to get the accounting done, that it's a voluminous accounting (3600 pages), that they aren't ignoring the Court, and that it's a 10-year accounting. Mr. Bassi stated that they requested an accounting 6/12/18, that they were to mediate, and that when there was no mediation that he filed the petition to remove the Trustee. Mr. Der stated that they were 3/4 of the way done with the accounting and that they then get an objection from Ms. Iwamura. Mr. Bassi stated that Mr. Tanaka isn't keeping good records, that Mr. Louie demanded an accounting and never got one, and that his client is entitled to an accounting as a beneficiary. Mr. Louie provided a brief history and addressed Roger Tanaka's Estate along with the issue of there being a need to be a "real party in interest". The Court stated that there isn't an issue regarding a party's standing before it today and that there are ways to raise that issue. Mr. Louie stated that his client wants an accounting, that they are okay with a 60-day continuance, that Dennis should be removed, and that Audrey is next in line & should be appointed. The Court stated that there was no petition for that before it today. Mr. Der stated that they have receipts that go back to 2008, that the primary accounts were at Union Bank, that there were some smaller accounts, that they have 2008 to 2009, that they don't have 2009 when the smaller accounts were transferred into Union Bank, and that the amounts in the stray accounts are less than $50,000.00. The Court stated that just a production was ordered, that there may be a need to amend the accounting petition re: loose ends with regard to the $50,000.00, and that everyone knows where the bulk of things stand now. Mr. Der stated that is why he suggested the spreadsheets. The Court stated that a dump of raw data is less responsive then doing as close to an accounting as you can, that it understands that time is needed to shape the data into an accounting, that the info gathering for that task has been done, that it needs to get into probate court format, and that it is not inclined to remove the Trustee. The Court noted that it was Mr. Tanaka's obligation all along to respond and account. The Court then continued the matter to April 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it already ordered an accounting in January, that Mr. Tanaka is to produce his best possible account in probate format by 3/19/19 which is to be based on Mr. Der's representations in court today, and that a status report is to be filed by 4/3/19 addressing what efforts are being made to collect the information & setting forth an "ETA" for the amended accounting. The parties were ordered to meet and confer regarding the accountings prior to 4/9/19. The Court stated that the matter is continued with the understanding that if Mr. Tanaka is not responding more receptively that there may be a change in management.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-05-2019 Continued To Mar-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Appoint Successor Trustee, Compel Accounting, And Compel Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-11-2018 Continued To Feb-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi for Marilyn Tanaka (Petitioner).
Ernest Der for Dennis Tanaka (Objector/Successor Trustee).

Mr. Bassi stated that both Counsel agree that the parties want to move towards distribution, that they asked for an accounting in June, that they still have not received it, and suggested a continuance to receive a hard copy of the accounting and review it. Mr. Der stated that he can get the accounting to them in 30-days, that the first accounting is for six or seven years since 2009 forward, that they are working on getting statements to bring it current and that he has about 2000 pages of documents for Mr. Bassi. The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to February 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be provided by January 29, 2019. Mr. Bassi is to provide an order with respect to the accounting and bifurcating all of the other issues. Said order is to be provided to Mr. Der for approval as to form.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.
